rule: hit = (kw>=2) OR (sem>=0.7) OR (func>=0.7); per-result independent scoring; total_hits is count of results triggering hit; verdict FAIL if total_hits >= 1, else PASS.
candidate_summary: BIND-RUNE 2-token ligature conjunction merge: post-hoc augmentation of BPE/Tiktoken tokenizer with a FIXED PRE-ENCODE merge table of top-N high-MI token pairs; encode-time bigram fusion to a reserved id; decode-time split back to constituents; T_bind embedding = mean of constituent embeddings; no LLM retrain; targets 10-20% sequence shortening.

results_analysis.title: Vocabulary Customization for Efficient Domain-Specific LLM Deployment (2509.26124)
results_analysis.notes: EXACT TWIN of LLM-side mechanism: post-hoc vocab augmentation with new tokens, ~20% sequence shortening, efficiency-preserving; matches FIXED-PAIR + POST-HOC components.

results_analysis.title: Teaching Old Tokenizers New Words (2512.03989)
results_analysis.notes: Post-hoc tokenizer extension on pretrained models (no retrain) — same paradigm as BIND-RUNE.

results_analysis.title: Train It and Forget It: Merge Lists are Unnecessary for BPE Inference (2508.06621)
results_analysis.notes: Inference-time BPE handling matches decode-time logic scope; merge-list-free vocabulary inference.

results_analysis.title: Say Anything but This: Tokenizer Betrays Reasoning (2601.14658)
results_analysis.notes: Non-invasive post-hoc token id manipulation, matching no-retrain post-hoc component.

results_analysis.title: Reducing Tokenization Premiums for Low-Resource Languages
kw_matches: coalesce multi-token characters into single tokens
results_analysis.notes: Post-hoc coalescing of multiple tokens into single token — directly equivalent to BIND-RUNE bigram fusion.

results_analysis.title: Multilingual Tokenization Advances (AdaptiVocab)
results_analysis.notes: AdaptiVocab replaces tokens with n-gram tokens post-hoc on pretrained BPE — same fixed-pair post-hoc augmentation.

results_analysis.title: Vocabulary expansion for non-SentencePiece BPE tokeniser
results_analysis.notes: Vocabulary expansion for BPE pretrained models matches post-hoc augmentation.

results_analysis.title: One Tokenizer To Rule Them All (2506.10766)
results_analysis.notes: Multilingual tokenizer plasticity; func below 0.7 but kw>=2 and sem>=0.7 trigger hit.

verdict_sentence: BIND-RUNE fails novelty verification: 8 of 8 retrieved results trigger hits under the rule (kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7), with exact-twin prior art including Vocabulary Customization (2509.26124, func 0.92, ~20% sequence shortening via post-hoc vocab augmentation), Teaching Old Tokenizers New Words (2512.03989), AdaptiVocab n-gram token replacement, and post-hoc multi-token coalescing (2601.13328) — all instantiating fixed-pair, post-hoc, no-retrain tokenizer augmentation indistinguishable from the proposed LLM-side recombination.
